text: This is the VOA Special English Agriculture Report.
zh: [0] 这里是美国之音特别英语节目《农业报道》。

text: Researchers estimate that more than 2 billion people a year get diseases spread by animals.
zh: [1] 研究人员估计,每年有超过20亿人感染由动物传播的疾病。

text: More than 2 million of them die. Delia Grace is a veterinary epidemiologist,
zh: [2] 其中超过200万人因此死亡。Delia Grace是一位兽医流行病学家,

text: an expert in the spread of diseases involving animals.
zh: [3] 是研究涉及动物疾病传播的专家。

text: She is also a food safety expert.
zh: [4] 她同时也是一位食品安全专家。

text: She works at the International Livestock Research Institute in Kenya's capital, Nairobi.
zh: [5] 她在肯尼亚首都内罗毕的国际家畜研究所工作。

text: She says diseases transmitted between animals and people are called zoonoses.
zh: [6] 她说,在动物与人之间传播的疾病被称为人畜共患病。

text: She says more than 60 % of human diseases come from other vertebrate animals.
zh: [7] 她指出,超过60%的人类疾病来自其他脊椎动物。

text: Some of these diseases are common. Others are extremely rare.
zh: [8] 其中有些疾病很常见,有些则极为罕见。

text: Delia Grace says there are many different infection pathways for a person.
zh: [9] Delia Grace说,人感染疾病的途径有很多种。

text: Probably the most common one is for people to get sick from food.
zh: [10] 最常见的可能是通过食物致病。

text: Other transmission pathways include direct contact with animals.
zh: [11] 其他传播途径包括与动物的直接接触。

text: And some diseases can be transmitted through water or through the air.
zh: [12] 还有一些疾病可以通过水或空气传播。

text: Delia Grace says diseases like avian influenza
zh: [13] Delia Grace说,像禽流感

text: or mad cow disease have killed very few people.
zh: [14] 或疯牛病这样的疾病致死人数其实很少。

text: but she says they are of interest because some of them could kill a lot of people.
zh: [15] 但她说这些疾病之所以受到关注,是因为其中一些可能致使大量人员死亡。

text: They include the Spanish flu after the First World War
zh: [16] 比如第一次世界大战后的西班牙流感,

text: and HIV, the virus that causes AIDS.
zh: [17] 还有导致艾滋病的HIV病毒。

text: Delia Grace is the lead author of a new report
zh: [18] Delia Grace是一份新报告的主要作者,

text: called Mapping of Poverty and Likely Zoonoses Hotspots.
zh: [19] 该报告名为《贫困与潜在人畜共患病热点区域制图》。

text: She points out that poverty and disease are closely linked,
zh: [20] 她指出,贫困与疾病之间关系密切,

text: so preventing the transmission of animal diseases could help reduce poverty.
zh: [21] 因此预防动物疾病传播有助于减少贫困。

text: The report for Britain's Department of International Development
zh: [22] 这份为英国国际发展部撰写的报告

text: lists places where the diseases are most common.
zh: [23] 列出了这些疾病最常见的地区。

text: The report lists places where a disease has existed for a long time,
zh: [24] 报告列出了长期存在此类疾病的地区,

text: a so -called endemic zoonosis, as well as places with new threats.
zh: [25] 即所谓的地方性人畜共患病,也列出了出现新威胁的地区。

text: Delia Grace says things could get worse in the coming years
zh: [26] Delia Grace说,未来几年情况可能会更加严峻,

text: as meat production increases to feed a growing world population.
zh: [27] 因为随着全球人口增长,肉类产量也在增加。

text: High production farms often raise animals close together.
zh: [28] 高密度养殖场通常将动物聚集饲养。

text: Crowding can allow diseases to spread quickly.
zh: [29] 拥挤的环境会使疾病迅速传播。

text: Another concern is the use of antibiotics in food animals,
zh: [30] 另一个令人担忧的问题是在食用动物身上使用抗生素,

text: not only to prevent and treat diseases, but to increase growth.
zh: [31] 这不仅是为了预防和治疗疾病,也是为了促进生长。

text: The report says an incentive -based system to encourage safer methods of raising animals
zh: [32] 报告指出,建立一种激励机制以鼓励更安全的养殖方式,

text: could be more effective than increasing food inspections.
zh: [33] 可能比增加食品检查更为有效。

text: For example, some farmers could receive training
zh: [34] 例如,可以为一些农民提供培训

text: and other help that would lead to official approval of their products.
zh: [35] 以及其他帮助,从而使他们的产品获得官方认证。

text: For VOA Special English, I'm Mario Ritter.
zh: [36] 这里是美国之音特别英语节目,我是Mario Ritter。
